homepage.description.message: A universal particle engine integrating NetDEM, CFDDEM, and computational mechanics modules

homepage.hero.title.message: Particle simulation for granular media and fluid–solid systems

homepage.hero.lead.message: A unified engine for discrete elements, CFD–DEM coupling, and solid mechanics—scaled from laptops to clusters, with a Python surface for everyday workflows.

homepage.scope.text.message: Guides, examples, and reference material for using Phynexis—especially through its {pythonInterface}, which is the main integration path we document here. The high-performance C++ core is distributed under research licensing; see {aboutLink} for contact and acknowledgements.

homepage.pillars.subtitle.message: DEM contacts, coupled flows, heterogeneous shapes, and parallel execution.

homepage.pillars.card1.desc.message: Robust contact for spheres, meshes, level sets, and learned shape fields—with solvers suited to stiff and irregular assemblies.

homepage.pillars.card2.desc.message: Multiple CFD–DEM formulations for resolved and hybrid regimes, so you can move from dry granular packs to suspension-like flows.

homepage.pillars.card3.desc.message: Continuum and multiphysics hooks alongside hybrid OpenMP/MPI runs—one codebase from prototyping to batch jobs on HPC.

homepage.pillars.card4.desc.message: Optional GPU-aware workflows (e.g. PyTorch) for surrogate contacts and geometric representations when classical models are too costly.

homepage.outro.text.message: Collaboration, licensing, and acknowledgements: {aboutLink}. Or reach us directly:
